pattern: After sorting, only the two smaller sides need checking against the largest, so a fixed largest side plus a two-pointer scan counts valid triples in quadratic time.
hints: For three sorted lengths, the triangle inequality holds iff the two smaller ones sum to more than the largest.
Sort the array and fix the largest side; count pairs of smaller indices whose sum exceeds it.
Sort ascending. For each k as the largest side, run l=0, r=k-1: if nums[l]+nums[r] > nums[k], all indices between l and r work, so add r-l and decrement r, else increment l.

edge_inputs.description: zeros can never form a triangle
degenerate where sum equals third side is invalid
too few elements for any triple
all equal sides always form triangles
a single huge side dominates all others

stress.description: large array of random side lengths
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 200))
    nums = [rng.randrange(0, 1001) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
stress.note: values within [0,1000]; mix of valid and degenerate triples
